title: Chocolate on Carpet
stainChemistry: Same three-component chemistry as chocolate-cotton (protein + oil + tannin). Carpet-specific constraint: pooled liquid wicks into the pad, and heat / hot water during treatment sets the milk protein. Synthetic face yarn tolerates enzymes + NSD + 3% H₂O₂; wool narrows the lane. Protein-before-heat ordering is non-negotiable.
whyThisWorks: Scrape chocolate bulk off carpet pile. Cold NSD via damp cloth for starter pigment. Enzyme damp-cloth for milk protein. NSD pulls cocoa butter. 3% H₂O₂ oxidizes phenolic tannin shadow. Weighted dry towel between every pass keeps the pad clean.
defaultAssumption: Kid / party context. Assume synthetic face yarn. Wool escalates. Pad contamination is the invisible failure mode.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Dull blade, ice pack if soft chocolate
spottingProtocol.instruction: Scrape solid chocolate off with a dull blade. If the chocolate is soft / melted into the pile, chill with an ice pack (in plastic) briefly first — firm chocolate lifts, smearing chocolate grinds in.

spottingProtocol.equipment: NSD in cold water, damp cloth, weighted dry towel
spottingProtocol.instruction: Apply cold NSD via damp cloth. Press, lift, rotate. Never pour. Weighted dry towel extraction between passes.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Liquid Protein Spotter / enzyme pretreat in warm (30–38°C) water on damp cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Dampen cloth with enzyme solution, press on spot, dwell 15–20 min. Digests the milk protein matrix that holds cocoa butter + tannin to the fiber. Never hot.
Second NSD pass on now-exposed cocoa butter.
3% H₂O₂ drop-by-drop on residual brown tannin. Patch-test on colored carpet first.

spottingProtocol.equipment: NSD damp cloth, heavy dry towel + weight
spottingProtocol.instruction: NSD-damp clean pass. Weighted dry towel 30 min.
Fan. Inspect next day for wicking. Wicking = pad contamination = pro.

steps: 1. Scrape bulk. Chill softy chocolate with ice pack.
2. Cold NSD damp cloth + weighted extract.
warnings: Protein before heat — hot water cooks milk protein into pile + backing
Enzymes die above 50°C — warm water, not hot

homeSolutions.agent: Scrape Solid Chocolate Off, Chill If Soft
homeSolutions.instruction: Use a dull butter knife to lift any solid chocolate off the carpet. If it's still soft or melted into the pile, press an ice pack (in plastic) on it for 5 min to firm it up first. Firm chocolate lifts; smeared chocolate grinds in.

homeSolutions.agent: Cold Water + Dawn on a DAMP Cloth
homeSolutions.instruction: Dampen a white cloth with cool water + a drop of Dawn. Press on the spot, lift, rotate. Never pour on carpet.

homeSolutions.agent: Enzyme Pretreat on a Damp Cloth
homeSolutions.instruction: Dissolve an enzyme pretreat (OxiClean Versatile, Biz) in WARM water per the label. Dampen a cloth, press on the spot 15–20 min. Digests the milk protein in the chocolate.

homeSolutions.agent: Dawn Again for the Cocoa Butter
homeSolutions.instruction: After the enzyme pass, re-apply Dawn + cool water on a damp cloth to break up the now-exposed cocoa butter.

homeSolutions.agent: 3% Hydrogen Peroxide for Brown Shadow (Light Carpet)
homeSolutions.instruction: Dab 3% peroxide on the residual brown via a damp cloth. Patch-test on colored carpet first.
homeSolutions.safetyNote: Never mix peroxide with chlorine bleach or ammonia.

homeSolutions.instruction: Weighted dry towel 20–30 min between every wet pass. Then fan-dry the spot.
Wool carpet + chocolate = pro. Ring wicks back = pro. Two rounds without clearing = pro.

steps: 1. Scrape solid chocolate. Chill soft chocolate with ice pack.
2. Cold water + Dawn on DAMP cloth. Never pour.
3. Enzyme pretreat on damp cloth 15–20 min (WARM water).
4. Dawn damp cloth again for cocoa butter.
5. 3% peroxide on brown shadow (light carpet, patch-test).
6. Weighted dry towel between passes, fan-dry.
7. Escalate for wool, wicking, or plateau.
products: Heavy book or pan for weighted drying
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop for wool carpet, wicking back, patch-test fail, or two-round plateau.
warnings: Protein before heat — hot water cooks milk protein
Never mix peroxide with chlorine bleach or ammonia

neverDo: Never apply hot water before enzyme step — cooks the milk protein
fiberSensitivities: Nylon / olefin / polyester: tolerates standard chemistry

escalation.when: Wool carpet, wicking back, patch-test fails, or two rounds plateau.
escalation.whatToTell: "Chocolate on [nylon / wool / synthetic] carpet, age [fresh / overnight / older], home-treated with enzyme + Dawn + 3% H₂O₂ + weighted towel."

contextQuestions: Carpet fiber — nylon / olefin / polyester / wool?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Same combination chemistry as chocolate-cotton. Carpet adds pad-risk + hot-water-avoidance. Protein ordering non-negotiable.
